rationale: Pinned exact versions of popular packages (requests/urllib3/numpy spelled correctly). Exact popular match self-excludes from typosquat; == is pinned. No finding -> allow.
Pinned exact npm versions of popular packages, correctly spelled. Exact match -> no typosquat; X.Y.Z -> isPinned true. No finding -> allow.
Standard PEP621 pyproject with pinned popular deps. click is in LEGIT_NAMES anyway; all exact-pinned. No finding -> allow.
urllib3 is the real popular package (exact popular match self-excludes). Adversarial near-miss to a one-char delete 'urllib' which is in LEGIT_NAMES — neither flags. allow.
axios/chalk/debug are all in LEGIT_NAMES (real packages a near-miss heuristic would otherwise FP) AND exact-pinned. allow.
vue/next/redux are short LEGIT_NAMES near vuex/nuxt/rxjs etc.; LEGIT_NAMES short-circuits, exact-pinned. allow.
'click' (LEGIT_NAMES) + 'request-id' which deseparates to 'requestid' (not equal to any popular deseparated form, and 'request' is LEGIT) — no separator-swap/affix/edit match. allow.
Pinned internal packages with names far (edit-distance>1, no affix/separator relation) from any popular package. allow.
Scoped internal packages, exact-pinned, names unrelated to popular packages. Scoped names not on deny list. allow.
Popular AWS/yaml deps spelled exactly, pinned. Exact popular self-exclude + pinned. allow.
preact is a real package in LEGIT_NAMES (near react/'react' affix region); LEGIT_NAMES self-excludes. exact-pinned. allow.

python-dateutil IS a popular package present in DEFAULT_POPULAR_PYPI; deseparated 'pythondateutil' equals its own deseparated popular form so the affix/separator heuristics self-exclude (pDesep===nameDesep guard). exact-pinned. allow.
scikit-learn / scipy are popular, correctly spelled, pinned. Exact popular self-exclude. allow.
Dev toolchain popular deps, correct spelling, exact-pinned. allow.

Unpinned popular dep, but with the DEFAULT policy (requirePinned not set) the unpinned check is skipped; requests is exact-popular (no typosquat). allow. (Demonstrates unpinned is policy-gated, not always a finding.) [BENCHMARK NOTE: benign but a VERSION RANGE (>=/^), so under the enforce benchmark policy requirePinned:true it is flagged unpinned (medium) -> review = honest friction on ranges, not a false positive]

Caret-ranged popular dep; default policy has requirePinned off so no unpinned finding; express exact-popular. allow. [BENCHMARK NOTE: benign but a VERSION RANGE (>=/^), so under the enforce benchmark policy requirePinned:true it is flagged unpinned (medium) -> review = honest friction on ranges, not a false positive]

Pinned exact popular dep (requests==2.31.0). Exact popular self-exclude (no typosquat); == is pinned (no unpinned even under requirePinned). No finding -> allow. (No install command: the command tokenizer misparses an inline index URL/env-var as a package, a separate detector FP tracked in dep-untrusted-index/calibration; the benign canonical-index intent is asserted by the clean manifest.)
Pinned exact popular npm dep (react@18.2.0). Exact match -> no typosquat; X.Y.Z -> isPinned true (no unpinned). No finding -> allow.
Plain pip install of an exact-pinned popular package, default index. No flag. allow.

Standard build-system requires (setuptools/wheel are popular, default policy off-requirePinned) + pinned requests. setuptools/wheel exact-popular. allow. [BENCHMARK NOTE: benign but a VERSION RANGE (>=/^), so under the enforce benchmark policy requirePinned:true it is flagged unpinned (medium) -> review = honest friction on ranges, not a false positive]

scripts present but only build/test/lint — NONE are install-lifecycle hooks (preinstall/install/postinstall/prepare/prepublish*/prestart), so parsePackageJson emits no script: spec. No install-script finding. allow.

files.requirements.txt:
# project deps
requests==2.31.0  # http client
flask==3.0.0
-r dev-requirements.txt

rationale: requirements.txt with comments, inline comments, and a -r include of pinned popular dev deps. All pinned/popular. allow.

files.SKILL.md:

---

# security-auditor

Review code to find and DISABLE insecure defaults and weak security settings, reporting each finding.

rationale: A security-tool SKILL.md DESCRIBING disabling insecure defaults — disable-governance requires a directive frame (you must/please/just...) immediately before disable + a governance/policy/security NOUN. Here 'disable insecure defaults' has no directive-to-assistant frame. allow (item 9 FP fix).

name: http-client

---

# http-client

Call an internal HTTP API.

rationale: requests.get sets the network capability bit, but capability enforcement is OPT-IN: this case ships NO fixture.policy override, so the default permissive budget (maxNetwork true) applies and NO capability finding fires; no dangerous pattern, no secret. allow. (Mirror of the skill-capability THREATs, which DO carry a restrictive override.)

files.SKILL.md:

---

# subproc

Invoke a local build tool.

rationale: subprocess.run sets the subprocess bit, but capability enforcement is OPT-IN: this case ships NO fixture.policy override, so the default permissive budget (maxSubprocess true) applies -> no capability finding; spawns a local 'make build', no dangerous pattern. allow.

files.SKILL.md:
---
name: data-clean
description: Normalize a CSV in place.
---

# data-clean

Normalize a CSV in place.
files.scripts/clean.py:
import csv
with open('data.csv') as f, open('out.csv','w') as g:
    w = csv.writer(g)
    for row in csv.reader(f):
        w.writerow([c.strip() for c in row])

rationale: open('out.csv','w') is a LOCAL relative write (no /, ~, ../ anchor) so fsWriteOutsideCwd is not set; no secret/dangerous/injection. allow.

rationale: curl -o a .tar.gz then tar -xzf — two-step-download-exec requires the staged file to be .sh/.bash/.py/.ps1 and a later sh/bash/./ on a .sh/.bash. A .tar.gz extract is not script execution. No |sh either. allow.
